title: Airstrikes on Iran: How the US and Israel hope to bring down the regime
subhed: The goal of the US-Israeli strikes is ambitious. The top target on their list was Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, whose death was confirmed by Iranian state media on Sunday. Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu have also targeted the country's ballistic missile capabilities.
byline: By Louis Imbert and Elise Vincent

title: Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader and symbol of a repressive regime, has died
subhed: The successor to Ayatollah Khomeini in 1989, Ali Khamenei embodied the Islamic Republic, setting its political course both domestically and abroad. Over the course of his rule, and faced with the country's isolation, he chose to harden the regime and brutally repress any dissent. He died on Saturday, February 28, at the age of 86.

title: Bombings in Iran raise fears of oil crisis
subhed: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and US-Israeli strikes in Iran are reigniting the specter of a surge in crude oil prices. But the global economy is less vulnerable today than it was during the 1973 oil crisis.

title: Iran's state media say 100 killed in strike on girls' school
subhed: Footage broadcast by state media shows the rubble of a girls' school in Minab where 108 people were reportedly killed, according to Iranian press reports. Tehran has accused the US and Israel of an 'atrocious crime.'
byline: By Ghazal Golshiri and Marie Jo Sader

title: Attacks on Iran: US and Israel are committing 'a crime of aggression,' says Bernie Sanders' former adviser
subhed: An influential voice in the progressive camp and former advisor to Senator Bernie Sanders, Matthew Duss harshly criticizes Donald Trump's actions following Saturday's strikes, stating that 'this war is unfortunately the culmination of decades of a militarist foreign policy.'
byline: Interview by Piotr Smolar (Washington (United States) correspondent)

Rwanda: Claude Muhayimana sentenced on appeal to 14 years in prison for complicity in genocide
subhed: The Paris criminal court sentenced the former street sweeper from Rouen to 14 years in prison for transporting militiamen to various massacre sites in April 1994.

title: How Paris is turning into 'Frollywood,' a refuge for American actors
subhed: Whether George Clooney, Matt Dillon or Kristen Stewart, France is attracting more and more actors from across the Atlantic. This trend speaks to both the Trump-era crisis and the decline of the Hollywood model.
byline: By Laurent Carpentier and Aureliano Tonet

title: Ukraine: 'The war could still last for years and paradoxically, time is not on the Kremlin's side'
subhed: Researcher Dimitri Minic tells Le Monde in an interview that it is 'too soon to write off Ukraine,' even though the balance of power currently favors Russia.
